hero.title: Your CTA shouldn't wait behind a chat widget.
hero.lead: Tag each block with how much it matters — for sales, search, or clicks — and biagiojs decides what hits the wire first, what hydrates, and what stays plain HTML. No islands on a page? That's zero JavaScript, full stop.
hero.schedNote: real build order from your weights

scores.title: We audited this exact page
scores.lead: Google Lighthouse on the site you're looking at — mobile on throttled 4G, desktop emulated. One small island; the rest is static HTML. The numbers speak for themselves.

band.a: zero JS unless you earn it
band.c: AVIF, WebP & JPEG out of the box

scheduler.title: Scheduler
scheduler.text: High-value sections climb the HTML source before heavy widgets. You declare what matters; the build reshuffles the deck.

islands.title: Islands
islands.text: Hydrate now, later, or never. A 300 KB chat bubble at low conversion stays static — your checkout doesn't wait for it.

seo.title: SEO
seo.text: Canonical URLs, Open Graph, JSON-LD, sitemap and hreflang — generated at build time, not bolted on later.

images.title: Images
images.text: Responsive AVIF, WebP and JPEG via sharp. Sensible defaults, per-image profiles, and a guardrail if you reference a file that doesn't exist.

ssg.title: SSG + adapters
ssg.text: Static sites by default. When you need SSR or ISR, adapters for Node, Vercel and Cloudflare are a config away.

demo.title: Move the sliders — watch the plan shift
demo.lead: There's one island on this page. Drag conversion and interaction: hydration moves live between static, lazy and eager. No reload, no guesswork.
demo.goldenText: Static is where we start. JavaScript is something you earn, not something we sprinkle by default.
demo.goldenHint: Rule of thumb: eager from ~0.3, lazy from ~0.05 — below that, the page stays JS-free.

code.title: Say what matters — the build does the rest

why.eyebrow: How we think about the web
why.title: Fast by default, deliberate when it counts

a.title: Static is the happy path
a.text: Most pages don't need client JavaScript. biagiojs assumes that until a component proves otherwise — so the fast case isn't something you have to fight for.

b.title: Business context drives the build
b.text: Conversion, SEO and interaction aren't metrics you chase after launch. They're inputs: the scheduler turns them into source order and a hydration plan you can reason about.

c.title: Real usage feeds the next build
c.text: CrUX field data, analytics and heatmaps land in reports/ and nudge the weights over time — the site learns how people actually use it, not how we guessed they would.

cta.title: Scaffold a project in one command
cta.text: Add a few weights to your components, run the build, and see what lands first on the wire. You'll know in minutes if the model fits your site.

footer.line: built with biagiojs — you're looking at the output of biagio build docs

docsIndex.title: Documentation
docsIndex.intro: Reference for building sites with biagiojs. Each page is static HTML from the same toolchain.

meta.homeTitle: biagiojs — put what matters first on the web
meta.homeDesc: A business-first framework for sites that should load fast and convert. You declare weights; biagiojs orders HTML, hydration and preload. Static by default, islands when they earn it.
meta.docsDesc: Guides and reference for biagiojs: getting started, .biagio syntax, business weights, adaptive hydration, automatic SEO, images, deploy and CLI.
